reviews.ownerResponse:
A requirement of California Law is that before a divorce can be finalized the court requires both spouses to complete documents stating the amount of property that they own and have to discuss dividing as well as stating how much income they make. The court then requires the spouses to exchange these documents so that the spouses can make a knowing and informed decision on how to divide their property and resolve their case. The California courts will not allow you to complete your divorce unless you and your spouse complete these documents and exchange them with your spouse. When the attorney and the Judge have to repeatedly order the other party to complete these forms and the other party refuses to do so, not only does it make their divorce case more expensive and if the client refuses to abide by the terms of the retainer agreement the attorney eventually has to request to be relieved from the case.

Further explanation is not possible because of attorney confidentiality requirements but it is never our while it is never our desire to withdraw from a case, there are instances where that action is necessary.

Thank you for your amazing 5-star review! We truly appreciate your trust in us to handle your personal injury case after such a traumatic experience. As a firm that fights for injured bicyclists in Carlsbad, we know how devastating these accidents can be, especially when a careless driver fails to respect cyclists’ rights on the road.

Bicycle Accidents Are a Major Problem in Carlsbad
Carlsbad is one of the most beautiful places in North County San Diego to ride a bike, but unfortunately, it’s also one of the most dangerous. With high-speed traffic, distracted drivers, and tourists unfamiliar with the area, accidents involving bicyclists happen far too often.

Some of the most dangerous areas for cyclists in Carlsbad include:

🚴‍♂️ Carlsbad Boulevard (Pacific Coast Highway/Highway 101) – This scenic coastal road is heavily traveled by both cyclists and vehicles. However, aggressive and distracted drivers make it a hotspot for serious bicycle crashes.
🚴‍♂️ Tamarack Avenue & Carlsbad Boulevard Intersection – A high-traffic area where cars often fail to yield to bicyclists in crosswalks or bike lanes.
🚴‍♂️ Palomar Airport Road & El Camino Real – Heavy commuter traffic and high speeds create a dangerous mix for cyclists.
🚴‍♂️ Cannon Road near the Agua Hedionda Lagoon – A popular cycling route with narrow shoulders and speeding vehicles.
🚴‍♂️ Grand Avenue & Carlsbad Village Drive – Heavy pedestrian and vehicle traffic make it a dangerous location for bicyclists, especially with distracted drivers.

We have handled countless bicycle accident cases in Carlsbad and know exactly what it takes to hold reckless drivers and their insurance companies accountable.

How We Win Big Settlements for Injured Bicyclists
Bicycle accidents often result in severe injuries due to the lack of protection compared to motor vehicles. Even at low speeds, a collision can cause broken bones, traumatic brain injuries, spinal injuries, and permanent disabilities. We understand how devastating these injuries can be, and we fight to recover the maximum compensation possible for:

✔ Medical Bills – Emergency care, hospital stays, surgeries, rehabilitation, and future treatment costs.
✔ Lost Wages – Compensation for time missed from work due to injuries.
✔ Pain & Suffering – The physical and emotional toll of an accident.
✔ Property Damage – Reimbursement for damaged bicycles and gear.
✔ Long-Term Disabilities & Scarring – Permanent injuries that affect a cyclist’s quality of life.

Our firm works with top medical specialists, accident reconstruction experts, and bicycle safety professionals to build a strong case and prove that our clients deserve top-dollar settlements.

Insurance Companies Fear Us – Here’s Why
Insurance companies try to blame cyclists or lowball injury claims, but we don’t let them get away with it. We have built a reputation for winning and are known throughout Carlsbad as aggressive negotiators who won’t back down.

✔ We Conduct Full Investigations – We gather traffic camera footage, witness statements, and expert opinions to prove driver negligence.
✔ We Work With Top Experts – Accident reconstructionists and medical professionals help us strengthen our case and maximize compensation.
✔ We Are Trial-Ready – If the insurance company refuses to offer a fair settlement, we take them to court and win big for our clients.
✔ We Fight Against Common Insurance Tactics – If an insurance company tries to claim our client was at fault or their injuries aren’t serious, we shut them down fast with solid evidence.

Why Cyclists in Carlsbad Choose Us
We are proud to be one of the top-rated personal injury law firms in North County San Diego with a proven track record of success in bicycle accident cases.

✅ Local Knowledge – We understand Carlsbad’s roads, traffic patterns, and the challenges cyclists face.
✅ Award-Winning Legal Representation – We’ve been recognized as a top-rated firm for personal injury cases.

Thank you so much for your 5-star review and for trusting us to handle your case. At our law office, we know that injuries aren’t always as straightforward as they may seem. Even in cases where a client has pre-existing injuries, we stand by the principle that every injury deserves to be fully compensated—and we use the Eggshell Plaintiff Doctrine to make sure that happens.

Understanding the Eggshell Plaintiff Doctrine
The Eggshell Plaintiff Doctrine is a legal principle that holds that a defendant must take their victim as they find them. This means that even if a person has a pre-existing injury or a condition that makes them more vulnerable to injury, the defendant is still fully liable for the extent of the harm caused by their actions. In simpler terms, if someone with a pre-existing condition (such as a back injury, joint issues, or even a previous accident) is involved in a car accident, they don’t lose their right to full compensation just because their condition made them more susceptible to injury.

For example, let’s say a person who has chronic back pain is in a car accident. While the crash may have been minor for most, it can cause serious damage to the person with the pre-existing condition. According to the Eggshell Plaintiff Doctrine, the defendant who caused the crash is still fully responsible for all the injuries, even if the victim’s injuries are more severe than what would typically result from the accident.

How This Helps Our Clients with Pre-Existing Injuries
When clients with pre-existing injuries come to us for help after an accident, we immediately look for ways to use the Eggshell Plaintiff Doctrine to their advantage. Many injured victims assume that a prior injury or condition will hurt their case, but that’s not true. The insurance companies will often argue that your previous condition caused the injuries, not the accident itself. This is where our expertise and aggressive tactics come in.

We understand the complexities involved in cases like these and know that insurance companies will try to use any excuse to reduce their payout. They’ll claim that the accident wasn’t the real cause of your pain and suffering, or that the pre-existing condition is what made the injury worse. But we won’t allow that. We work closely with top medical professionals and accident reconstructionists to prove that the accident was the substantial cause of the injuries—and that you deserve full compensation for your pain and suffering.

Fighting for the Best Settlement, Despite Pre-Existing Conditions
One of the key reasons we are successful in fighting cases involving pre-existing injuries is our deep understanding of how to present a case that acknowledges the pre-existing condition but still places the responsibility squarely on the defendant. We never allow the defendant to shift blame to your prior injuries or conditions. We have the experience and know-how to:

✔ Document the extent of the pre-existing injuries – We work with specialized medical professionals who can thoroughly document how the accident aggravated your existing condition or created new injuries. This includes working with orthopedic surgeons, neurologists, chiropractors, and physical therapists to get a complete picture of how the accident made things worse.
✔ Gather evidence that shows how the defendant’s negligence (whether it was speeding, distracted driving, or recklessness) led to the injuries. Even if the injuries might have been less severe for someone without a pre-existing condition, the accident still exacerbated the problem. We make sure the full extent of your injuries is clear to the jury, the judge, and the insurance company.
✔ Ensure that you are compensated for future medical care – Soft tissue injuries and aggravated conditions often require long-term care. By partnering with your healthcare providers, we ensure that we accurately estimate the future medical costs you will incur due to the injury’s lasting effects.

We know how devastating it is to suffer a severe back injury, especially when it requires surgery and long-term recovery. Your case was a tough battle, but we were honored to fight for you and ensure you received the compensation you truly deserved.

Back Injuries After a Car Accident – Life-Changing and Painful
Back injuries are some of the most serious and debilitating injuries a person can suffer in a car crash. The spine is incredibly complex, and when it’s injured, it can lead to:
✔ Herniated discs – Causing severe pain, numbness, and mobility issues
✔ Spinal fractures – Requiring surgical intervention and months of recovery
✔ Nerve damage – Leading to chronic pain, weakness, and loss of function
✔ Ongoing medical treatment – Including physical therapy, pain management, and in severe cases, spinal fusion or other surgical procedures

In your case, we knew that the insurance company would do everything possible to minimize your claim, arguing that back injuries are common and trying to lowball your settlement. We refused to let them get away with that.

Proving the Need for Back Surgery – A Tough Fight Against Insurance Companies
One of the biggest challenges in cases involving spinal injuries is proving just how serious they are. Insurance companies love to argue that back pain is pre-existing, degenerative, or could have been treated without surgery.

That’s why we worked with top spine specialists and surgeons to document every detail of your injury. We made sure that:
✅ MRI scans and medical reports clearly showed the damage to your spine
✅ Expert medical testimony proved that surgery was necessary for your recovery
✅ Physical therapy and pain management records demonstrated the severity of your condition

With this solid medical evidence, we were able to dismantle the insurance company’s arguments and force them to acknowledge the full extent of your injuries.

The Financial Impact of Back Surgery – Getting You the Maximum Settlement
Back surgery is not cheap, and we made sure that every cost was included in your claim, including:
✔ Hospital bills, surgical costs, and anesthesia
✔ Post-surgical rehabilitation and physical therapy
✔ Pain management treatments and medications
✔ Lost wages and reduced earning capacity due to the long recovery period
✔ Future medical costs for continued treatment and care

We fought aggressively to ensure that you didn’t have to worry about how to pay for your medical care—the negligent driver’s insurance company was held fully accountable.

Why Insurance Companies Fear Us
Insurance companies fear personal injury lawyers who are willing to go the distance, and that’s exactly what we do. When they tried to lowball your case, we made it clear that we were prepared to file a lawsuit and take them to trial.

Because of our reputation for aggressive litigation and our track record of winning high-dollar settlements, they had no choice but to pay what your case was truly worth rather than risk an even bigger loss in court.

Helping You Move Forward After a Life-Changing Injury
We know that no amount of money can take away the pain and stress of what you went through. But what we could do—and what we DID do—was make sure that you got the resources you need to focus on healing and rebuilding your life.

Your 5-star review means the world to us, and we are so glad we could be there for you during such a difficult time. If you or anyone you know ever faces a similar situation, we’re here to fight for them too.

Wishing you the best in your recovery!

One of the biggest challenges in semi-truck accident cases is dealing with the massive insurance policies these companies carry. In fact, their number one goal is to minimize your claim and offer the lowest possible settlement. Whether a client is suffering from whiplash, spinal cord injuries, broken bones, traumatic brain injuries, or chronic pain, we ensure that every aspect of their suffering is accounted for in their settlement.

Accidents involving semi-trucks, 18-wheelers, and commercial delivery vehicles can leave victims with injuries that affect them for the rest of their lives. Many suffer from crippling back pain, herniated discs, nerve damage, and mobility issues that make it impossible to return to work. Rear-end collisions with semi-trucks are particularly devastating because of the sheer size and weight of these vehicles. Victims of these accidents often face months—if not years—of rehabilitation, and we make sure their settlement reflects the true cost of their recovery.

The insurance companies know that settling with us is their best option because we are fully prepared to take a case to trial if they don’t offer a fair amount. We don’t settle for lowball offers. Our firm has handled countless cases in Vista, San Marcos, Oceanside, Carlsbad, and surrounding areas, and we have helped accident victims recover millions of dollars in settlements and verdicts.

At Brothers in Law Office, we believe that every client deserves a fierce advocate who will stand up to the big insurance companies and fight for justice. Military Law Center helped me during one of the worst times in my career. They were upfront, kept things simple, helped me do the work, got me answers, and assisted when I needed it. When my case ended, the disengagement was smooth and simple. I would absolutely recommend them to anyone facing the military legal system.  Don't take any chances on your career. Thanks again, Kevin.

Gary saved my Navy career. I retained him to represent me in a military sexual harassment case in which I was the target of workplace mobbing and ostricism. From the very beginning, Gary and his team displayed exceptional professionalism, compassion, and an unmatched mastery of military law.

Through his meticulous review of my case—which included several pages of documented evidence—Gary uncovered major discrepancies in the investigation. He discovered that 19 witnesses had provided testimony, yet only eight of those statements had been turned over to me. The Navy had withheld 11 witness statements, something I never would have known without his thoroughness and determination to uncover the truth. He also identified that the investigating officer assigned to my case was neither current on applicable directives nor qualified to conduct the investigation in the first place.

Gary’s expertise and attention to detail applied real pressure to my chain of command, even during the NJP process, where it became undeniable that my case had been mishandled. Thanks to his strategic guidance and relentless advocacy, I was able to preserve my life's work. Although I didn’t receive the full “not guilty” finding I had hoped for, the suspended bust and restriction were a far better outcome than separation—which could have ended everything I had worked for over 15 years of military service.

Gary and his staff were consistently supportive, patient, and encouraging, especially during moments when I felt completely defeated. Their commitment never wavered. I’m deeply grateful that I chose him to represent me. If you’re facing military legal issues, don’t hesitate—Military Law Center is the team you want.

I am a combat veteran, with 10 years of service. In the fall of 2020, I received a call from the Public Defender’s office asking me if I was going to use their services. I told her that she must have the wrong number. She confirmed that she in fact had the correct number. She went on to tell me I was being indicted on two felony counts for attempting to exercise my 2nd Amendment right to purchase a shotgun. I thought, “This is some kind of joke.” It was not.
In the summer of 2016, I was taken to a hospital by the police for an alcohol related incident. Prior to leaving the hospital for home, I apparently signed a document acknowledging that I could not purchase  a firearm for 5 years. I don’t remember this or really anything about that night.
After becoming sober, four years later in December 2019, I attempted to purchase a shotgun so I could teach my son to shoot.  On the 7th day of the 10-day waiting period I received a phone call that the gun store could not release the weapon to me. I didn’t know why. Two months later I was contacted by an investigator asking why I tried to purchase a gun. “You are barred from owning a firearm” he said. This was news to me. He explained that almost four years earlier I was given what’s called a 5150. This means that a doctor had determined that I am a danger to myself and others. I don’t remember ever hearing those words from a doctor.
I was given a public defender who, in my opinion, did nothing but try to intimidate me into taking the prosecutor’s deal. I refused because I honestly did not know that I was a barred person and was willing to fight for my 2nd amendment right, even if that meant I might become a convicted felon. It seemed like the DA’s office and the public defender both just wanted me to go away. I was getting irritated with the way the whole situation was developing.
Finally, I was fed up. I started asking around for any effective attorneys who truly believed in our constitutional rights and that wouldn’t try to extend the case in order to increase their bill. A veteran neighbor suggested Attorney Gary Barthel at the Military Law Center. Mr. Barthel is a veteran himself and so I felt he could understand the nuance of this case. What he discovered blew my mind.
After retaining Mr. Barthel, he immediately started to examine the evidence and determined the 5150 determination, that I was threat to myself or others, was not properly assessed in accordance with California law.  Additionally, Mr. Barthel also determined I was denied a required hearing to appeal California’s 5-year ban to purchase a weapon.  Based upon the results of his investigation, Mr. Barthel was able to convince the District Attorney’s office to dismiss the charges.
It took Mr. Barthel  only a few months to get all charges dropped. Attorneys are expensive so it is important to find one that is efficient. Mr. Barthel was able to win the case and since it was done so quickly, my total bill was very reasonable. I was also refunded the unused portion of the retainer. I don’t plan on ever needing a criminal defense attorney again, but if I do, I’m calling Gary Barthel. He is very competent, and I would highly recommend his services.
Smart guys & great results! Nick & Gary solved couple year old 'problem' I'd gotten into in Riverside- THANKS

reviews.text:
I would give them 0 stars if I could, I paid for my consultation of 350 Dlls, I attended my consultation and was attended by another person who was not the lawyer they recommended to me, they told me that the 350 Dlls that the consultation cost would be discounted from the total cost of the process and I accepted, the truth is that it seemed very expensive for a consultation but I was recommended by a relative, that's why I agreed to pay and I decided to do my process with them. It's been approximately 2 or almost 3 months since the time I decided to do my process here and they agreed to call me, I have called a total of approximately 10 times reminding them that no one has called me and asking that the lawyer please call me to start my process and they have never called me, they say that they will call me when they are free but they do not call me, it's not fair I asked for a refund for the consultation but they say that it is non-refundable, ok but nobody said that they would only charge me for the consultation and then they would not do my Immigration process. I give them 0 stars. Please don't waste your time with them. They've only delayed my process and wasted my time.
I hope this helps someone. Immigration processes are really long, so don't let the lawyer delay the process any further.

reviews.text: Highly recommend this firm and team to anyone who needs a lawyer!  The Intake Specialist Perla was very helpful and made me feel comfortable by taking all the stress from me and my family!

These guys are awesome. It was a tough thing to get hurt and not know what to do but Eugene put me at ease right away and took care of everything. I would definitely recommend these guys if you need an accident attorney.

I entrusted my case to these lawyers and they did not disappoint. I am very pleased with how they treated me as a client and received a settlement that I believe was very fair considering what happened to me. I would recommend this law firm to friends and family without hesitation. You can trust them.
Bassem Salem was phenomenal. He was very patient and kind with me every step of the way. I recommend him to anyone dealing with a car accident case.

I was visiting San Diego when I got hurt in a car accident. Everyone told me I needed a San Diego lawyer, but I was hesitant due to the distance and not knowing any lawyers personally. Getting a San Diego lawyer was the right advice and hiring the Bruno law firm in San Diego was the absolute right choice! Gene and Brian are both transplanted New Yorkers. Everyone knows how tough New Yorkers are — and these guys are pit bulls! I felt like I was in the right hands right from the start and they were there for me right up until I got a fat settlement check. Bruno, you guys are the best!

I'd like to thank the entire BRUNO team, specifically Perla Alex and Brian. Their honesty and transparency during this entire process has been amazing. Perla was very optimistic and encouraging and my attorney Brian and case manager Alex were amazing with the updates and clarification and ultimately a great settlement too. The communication and team work these guys provided me was amazing! The BRUNO team are the best injury lawyers in North County.

Estate planning felt overwhelming until I met with them in February. I just wanted to ensure my family's safety, but I had no concept about the paperwork needed. They took the time to clarify legal language in plain English, which I greatly appreciated. Now that everything has been finalized and filed, I can finally sleep well at night.

I decided it was time to put proper estate planning documents in place and met with Marc at County Law Center to discuss my options. He explained how a will, powers of attorney, and beneficiary designations work together. He drafted the documents to reflect my specific circumstances rather than using a standard template. The overall process was straightforward and well organized.

After struggling with significant debt, I scheduled a consultation with a bankruptcy attorney at County Law Center to understand my options. Mr. Duxbury reviewed my income, assets, and liabilities before recommending the appropriate course of action. He prepared a petition to the court and explained the timeline of the filing. The matter was handled efficiently and with attention to detail. Thank you!

County Law Center is the best Law office to help with the difficult process of divorce and family services. They were there to answer questions, give advice and ensure I understood my rights, while making sure I was kept in the loop on each step along the way. They will listen to you, fight for you and ensure you are supported through a very stressful process. I highly recommend Marc Duxbury and his team of professionals when you need the best.

reviews.text: Marc has been my family law attorney since the beginning of 2017.  I never had an attorney, especially one to help me get more time with my daughter in the state of California.  To this very day, I have been beyond fortunate to have Marc in my corner to fight for my time with my daughter.  As we just finished another round of family court, he is clear, patient, and understanding to bring options and resolutions to make sure that my daughter can flourish and I can be happy with the end result.  Marc is a father like I am so I can honestly say that he understands the importance of parenting especially between two divorced parents.  Divorce isn’t easy, but going to court and fighting for your time with your child is worse, especially for a father!  However, because I have Marc in my corner, I don’t have a problem going back to court at any time because he is there.  He will not sugar coat things because law does not work that way. reviews.text:
Two points for taking my call.

I called the county law center this morning gave them an overview of what I needed they assured me they could deal with it. I spoke to I'm assuming the main lawyer. After 15 seconds, and I will admit I was a little emotional due to the surrounding circumstances, he interrupted me and said he didn't want to take my case because he was saddled with work. Sir, then why take my call only to turn me down?

Again this might be my own doing because I was slightly emotional on the phone as you might be able to understand when you're dealing with trusts and family and a negative situation. The gentleman might be an absolute professional and stud in the ring but getting his services might be tricky.

Was given 30 days continuance to serve me. I had to call twice and write a formal letter. Even went to his office to pick up his clients pleadings, and he wasn't there. Not my job btw. He finally served the papers 3 days before our hearing by mail, when it's supposed to be 21 days. Needless to say I wasn't happy and wasn't able to file my response in time.
Always been reliable, one on one, and never have I had I ran into any issues with your service. If you need an attorney Marc is who you should call.

reviews.text:
We had used a lawyer friend of many years to implement our estate planning and its revisions over the years. After moving we were concerned about finding a competent person to replace her.
We were initially referred to an individual who was retiring and that person re-referred us to Opelon LLP; in other words, we were going in pretty much blind.  You can't imagine my surprise upon meeting the proprietor, Owen Rassman!
Owen's father is a physician with whom I worked many years ago in the Los Angeles area where Owen the child would occasionally appear at the office.  Because I was well-acquainted with the  very upright ethical behavior of his father,  I was fairly certain that Owen would be a principled and ethical attorney thereby obviating my primary concern about divulging such significant personal information to a complete unknown.
My other major worry, competence, was put to rest post haste. His initial presentation covered all conceivable issues, potential problems and their resolution so thoroughly that my crib sheet of questions (scoured from the Internet after googling "estate planning") became superfluous.  In the end we were educated and walk through the process step-by-step and left the office feeling confident and worry-free.
If you are concerned, as you should be, about the importance and intricacies of estate planning,  I think you'll find  your mind put at ease by consulting with Owen Rassman at Opelon LLP.
I heartily recommend him without reservation.
Mark Andrews
reviews.ownerResponse: Mark, wow! First, what a great pleasure and surprise to get re-acquainted with you after so many years had passed; truly my pleasure. Second, re: your review, thank you! One of the joys of my profession is putting clients' minds at ease. Please don't hesitate to reach out if I can be of any further assistance! Wishing you the best, Owen Rassman

reviews.text: We've hired & dealt with various attorneys since as far back as the early 80's on any number of issues, concerns & projects. We've learned that each do come with various degrees of expertise, skill, competence, ethics & honesty. As the final trustee I hired this skilled wordsmith lawyer to look over our final 2015 restatement of our family trust after my mother's passing just to make sure we crossed all our t's & dotted all our i's. I did so even though the San Francisco attorney who originally wrote this trust for my parents was already in the process of reviewing it with me. Let me tell you how thankful I am I had the foresight to do so. The very 1st thing you'll learn about dealing with & learning from Paul is he actually 'listens' to you before he ever begins to research, advise or relay his expert knowledge and/or opinion to you. Rather a rare & lost quality these days especially in this profession that I haven't always had the highest opinion of but full disclosure here, Paul has absolutely NO IDEA I'm posting this (5) five-star review here for him. As someone who wouldn't in a heartbeat neglect complain or object when someone doesn't deliver what they promise I must acknowledge what professional & sound advice he gave us here to not only handle our other family members but also the knowledge & foresight to contend & deal with the original fast talking attorney who obviously wanted to drag this entire ordeal out as long as possible for obvious reasons.  Yes, I will & would recommend this legal professional to any & all who would ask & personally plan to use him again if he'll put up with me moving forward. Do yourself a favor & give his office a call if you're ever in the market for a knowledgeable honest attorney concerning this issue(s) & learn for yourself if what I've stated here isn't absolutely true.
reviews.ownerResponse: Bob, I am blushing.  Jan and I have truly enjoyed the opportunity to be of service.  We try to exceed our clients expectations.  You made it easy for us, thanks to your cooperation and desire to do the best for your family.

reviews.text:
Intellectually, I knew that I should have a will, a Trust, a designated power of attorney, healthcare proxy, and everything else that goes into a complete estate plan. Emotionally, I didn't want to think about "afterward" and put it off for years. When my dad passed recently, I felt a new urgency to take care of "afterward" now.

Greg was terrific to work with, and I enjoyed working with him. I had been feeling somewhat anxious about the process and having to make decisions about things I didn't want to think about. Greg is empathetic to those feelings and put me at ease right from the start.

In addition to his extensive legal knowledge and expertise in estate planning, Greg is calm, patient, professional, and an excellent listener and communicator. He clearly and patiently explained to me and guided me through everything that needed to be done to make sure my estate will be managed in accordance with my wishes and to make sure my beloved fur-baby will continue to be loved and well-cared for by the person I want him entrusted to.

Greg made the entire process as easy as possible for me, which I highly value and deeply appreciate. He arranged for a Notary when we needed one, filed all the documents with the correct government office, and  presented my completed plan to me in a professional binder.

Greg, overall, lifted a heavy weight from my shoulders. Had I known how easy it would be working with Greg, I would have turned to him for help years ago.

I highly recommend Greg for estate planning.
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you so much for your kind and thoughtful words. It truly means a great deal to hear that your experience was a positive one, especially given how overwhelming the estate planning process can feel at the outset. reviews.ownerResponse: This is not my client.  I reported this review as fake.  Google turned down my request to have this fake review removed.  I have no idea who this person is, so I can't specifically respond.  I can only generally respond that I have been helping injured workers for the past 30 years, and I have helped more than 4,500 clients with excellent results.  The great majority (95% or more) of my clients are referrals from persons I helped in the past 30 years.  My clients refer me their family and friends all the time.  They make those referrals because they have been happy with my work.  I make it my priority to personally return all phone calls from my clients within a day or two from the day of the call.  I comprehensively address any concerns or questions they may have about their case.  Work Comp matters can involve many complex issues, and those issues lead to multiple trials on the record before a judge.  Litigation gives all parties due process.  Due process requires that time be given to both sides to fully respond and address the issue being litigated.  Such protracted litigation causes delays in cases and those delays can be very frustrating to injured workers.  For the most part, my clients understand I am litigating and fighting hard for their rights in their cases.  That is what I do every day of the week in my practice.  There is no magic wand that an attorney can wave and immediately fix everything immediately.  Things take work and time in real life.
Thank you for taking the time to give me such a nice review.  Your review will help me appear in more searches by persons in need of help with their Workers' Compensation claims.

reviews.text: Mike Fischer (and help from Andrea) at Fischer & Van Thiel represented me in my divorce. After firing my first attorney (I felt there was a lack of effort and caring), I searched/interviewed lawyers for  someone to listen to what was happening and take action. Mike listened, and he has been divorced as well.  Mike was able to explain the court processes, lay out a plan to achieve my goals, and focus on what matters. When it came to paperwork, Andrea helped tremendously by transcribing the important facts, responses and requests into well written and understandable legalese. Only a few of the contested divorce issues were agreed to in advance (an existing order was in place, but was not ideal). So when we finally had our day(s) in court (there is a lot of waiting in family law), Mike did a good job when responding to the opposing counsel, and he made a point to touch on the critical requests with the judge (like custody, phone call schedule, holidays, support, etc). Ultimately, with Mike's help, I was able to achieve my desired goals. I would highly recommend Fischer & Van Thiel.

reviews.ownerResponse:
A requirement of California Law is that before a divorce can be finalized the court requires both spouses to complete documents stating the amount of property that they own and have to discuss dividing as well as stating how much income they make. The court then requires the spouses to exchange these documents so that the spouses can make a knowing and informed decision on how to divide their property and resolve their case. The California courts will not allow you to complete your divorce unless you and your spouse complete these documents and exchange them with your spouse. When the attorney and the Judge have to repeatedly order the other party to complete these forms and the other party refuses to do so, not only does it make their divorce case more expensive and if the client refuses to abide by the terms of the retainer agreement the attorney eventually has to request to be relieved from the case.

Further explanation is not possible because of attorney confidentiality requirements but it is never our while it is never our desire to withdraw from a case, there are instances where that action is necessary.

reviews.text: Stephanie is a passionate tenant advocate and I would highly recommend her legal services. The County of San Diego needs hundreds of more lawyers like Stephanie.

I had an excellent experience working with Attorney Stephanie Germani on my landlord–tenant case. She is exceptionally professional, knowledgeable, and compassionate. Throughout the process, she was patient, responsive, and always took the time to explain every step clearly. I truly appreciated her dedication and genuine care for her clients — I could not have asked for better guidance. Highly recommend her services to anyone seeking a trustworthy and skilled attorney.

At a time when my partner and I had a lot going on, Stephanie’s legal expertise lifted a weight off our shoulders. We were having problems with our new landlord and property managers.  We were receiving improper buyout offers, harassment from third parties, and a sudden notice that we would have several additional monthly charges to our lease agreement. Immediately contacting Stephanie she explained our rights and possible solutions. She carefully crafted a letter on our behalf. It was written with such compelling citations to statues and regulations, that the positive effects were felt not only by my partner and I but also by our neighbors. reviews.text:
I was having trouble getting out of my lease. I had submitted a 30 day notice and my landlord wouldn’t let me go. I was informed by management, before signing my lease, that submitting a notice 30 days in advance would get me off the lease. Well when it was time to move out, the information that was given to me was wrong.

I was stressed, couldn’t sleep, and looking for help anywhere I could get it. Everyone kept telling me the same thing over and over again, that I couldn’t get out of it until both my roommate and I left together. Thankfully, a good friend of mine recommended Stephanie. He had worked with her in the past and said wonderful things. HE WASNT KIDDING.

Stephanie was helpful from the very beginning. I don’t understand housing law, I mean who does? I was asking Stephanie hundreds of questions and she answered every single one of them. I’ve always been intimidated by legal help, but Stephanie is very welcoming, kind, and patient. I can’t emphasize this enough, if you’re having legal housing trouble, she is the person to go to.

Within a week of her helping me, my landlord finally succumbed and agreed to let me off the lease. I can’t tell you how relieved I am to not worry about it anymore. Thank you very much Stephanie for all of your help. I’m definitely recommending you to all my friends and family.

Atty. Stephanie Germani helped me with my landlord/owner dispute for the rental house that we vacated. She was very knowledgeable about the law and very attentive to details. She patiently listened and read all the information that I gave her. She drafted a demand letter to the landlord/owner and they conceded. There was no time wasted on my part, no court appearances and, most importantly, no thousands of dollars lost to unreasonable demands from my previous landlord/owner. Thank you, Atty. Germani! You rock!

Stephanie helped me navigate a wrongful eviction summons from my previous landlord who had been foreclosed. She openly communicated everything that would be needed from me and was able to review my documents and assured me we could get the case dismissed. Sure enough, she did just that. She let me know she would be able to assist me further if things continued to escalate. I’m so grateful she was able to resolve this issue for me so quickly. I’ll be sure to reach out to her if I ever need legal representation as I’m confident I’m in good hands.

All I can say is WOW! I was referred to Stephanie by complete chance and luck and I honestly can't thank her enough for what she did for me. My previous apartment complex (wrongfully) kept my entire deposit and I tried to fight them on my own (with no luck). The minute I reached out to Stephanie she took my case seriously and spent the time to research and formulate a wonderful letter for me to send those crooks! I was so frustrated and having Stephanie there to just listen and validate my concerns meant more to me than anything. Thank you for the work you do, Stephanie, and it was a pleasure meeting you!
I am very pleased since they make it all so calm and easy. No stress. That is what we all need.

I really can't rave enough about this place. Doan law helped me through a really difficult time with needing to file for bankruptcy and I truly couldn't have asked for better service. I would highly recommend if you are a father and your doing the right thing for your child and have records for her to defend you then I would recommend her. reviews.text: Most of our communication was done via email. After putting down the large retainer we paid thousands over for a step parent adoption. We would pay the invoices without asking questions or for detailed itemizations. They kept adding up till the point that my uncle scratched his head due to the fact of we exceeded the typical cost of stepparent adoption. I did the research  it’s true. I believe we are getting over charged and being a military family income is limited. After paying another large balance in November I thought that was it. But no we wake up to almost another thousand due which I’m so confused as to why since the adoption happened over a month ago. They didn’t go to the court date to finalize and only sent us the adoption letter via mail since then? Work with people that won’t take advantage and over charge for every little thing. I wish we did more research instead of paying over thousands than the typical lawyer would charge for the same service.
reviews.ownerResponse: Hi Anna, Thank you for the review and for working with us to get your adoption finalized. We have helped hundreds of clients just like you and adoptions remains one of our passions. I appreciate your thoughtful feedback and the fact that you updated your rating to 4 star after we spoke. While I cannot divulge confidential information, I can indicate that contested adoptions can be very complicated and each presents unique circumstances and costs based on those circumstances. We strive to provide great service at a reasonable cost, including discounts for our military members. This often includes participating in adoption finalization hearings, but that is not always possible when other contested matters arise. As always, we continue to strive to improve our services and we will continue to do so. We hope you have continued to do well and have enjoyed your time with your family.

Original review:

Ali follows up, remembers details about small things, and the entire staff we’ve come in passing with have been kind and courteous. Down to the smallest details of having a sign that welcomes you in during signings. I’ve already referred my family here to ensure their estate is buttoned up.

10/10 recommend Brenda and her team for any of your trust, estate, business or otherwise legal needs.
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you, Denice! Your feedback made our day—thank you for sharing your great experience and for referring your family to us! We appreciate wonderful clients like you!
